constraints: Reviewer received only the supplied render images and task expectations.
Reviewer did not inspect code, manifests, or repository files.
Reviewer used anonymous labels A/B/C inside each supplemental case.

summary.key_finding: The supplemental stills are useful as failure evidence, but they do not support a successful three-way qualitative comparison panel.

per_image_reviews.label: A
per_image_reviews.visible_evidence: Blue close-up sphere with bright specular highlight; coated/glossy effect is visible.
per_image_reviews.main_risk: Object is cropped extremely tightly and appears faceted, reducing comparison quality.

per_image_reviews.label: B
per_image_reviews.visible_evidence: Same close framing as A, but sphere is plain white with little visible material character.
per_image_reviews.main_risk: Possible material fallback or loss of blue/clearcoat appearance.

per_image_reviews.label: C
per_image_reviews.visible_evidence: Only grid/axis lines are visible; no sphere/target appears.

per_image_reviews.label: A
per_image_reviews.visible_evidence: Flat target clearly shows dense checker/procedural pattern.
per_image_reviews.main_risk: Pattern is very dense and slightly alias-prone, but identifiable.

per_image_reviews.label: B
per_image_reviews.visible_evidence: Flat target is visible, but appears solid white with no checker pattern.

per_image_reviews.label: C
per_image_reviews.visible_evidence: Flat target is visible, but appears mostly beige/tan with no checker pattern.
per_image_reviews.main_risk: Missing procedural texture or baked result not visibly preserving the pattern.

panel_reviews.rationale: Clearcoat C shows only grid/axes and no target object, so the three-way panel is invalid. Clearcoat A/B are visible but tightly cropped.
The procedural camera framing is comparable, but existing_noMDL and NVIDIA conditions visibly lose the checker pattern.

retake_recommendation: Retake Clearcoat C with the sphere visible in the same framing as A/B.
For Clearcoat A/B, pull the camera back slightly so the full sphere silhouette is visible.
Retake Procedural B/C after confirming the checker/procedural material renders visibly; keep the current camera framing because it is comparable across the procedural set.

allowed: Use this as clean-room visual QA over the two supplemental missing-bin fixture cases.
Use it to justify retaking clearcoat framing and investigating procedural texture preservation before success claims.
Use NVIDIA clearcoat as a selected failure-case example when paired with static-gate and machine-QA evidence.
forbidden: Do not call the supplemental contact sheet a successful three-way qualitative comparison.
Do not call this a final material-effect error-rate distribution.
Do not describe the review as a human annotator study.

source_context.review_packet: Inspect six supplemental material-effect render images as a clean-room human-style visual QA reviewer. Clearcoat A/B/C and procedural A/B/C were anonymous labels mapped back after review.
